children.text: Waterside sits along Stamford’s shoreline and has a distinctly coastal character. It’s a neighborhood where the presence of the Sound is part of daily life—morning walks, time at the park, and a setting that feels different from Stamford’s inland neighborhoods.

Real estate in Waterside varies by location, but many streets feature mid-century single-family homes with an established, lived-in neighborhood feel. The overall atmosphere tends to be relaxed and outdoors-forward, and it works well for buyers who want water proximity without committing to a purely resort-style enclave.

John J. Boccuzzi Park is a major local anchor at 200 Southfield Avenue. The City describes it as a 12-acre coastal park with a playground, fields, a basketball court, two tennis courts, a beach, and a marina. For birdwatching and quiet time outdoors, Rosa Hartman Park on Brownhouse Road sits near the Greenwich border and is known locally as a birding spot. With downtown Stamford a short drive away, Waterside offers coastal living with practical access.

highlights: Coastal parks with beach, marina, and recreation facilities
Coastal Stamford setting with a relaxed, outdoors-forward lifestyle near Long Island Sound
Mix of home styles and price points for different buyer needs
Easy drive to downtown Stamford dining, arts, and Metro-North service
Birdwatching and nature areas in nearby parks
name: Waterside
overview: Waterside is one of Stamford’s coastal neighborhoods where residential streets and shoreline access come together. The housing mix includes mid-century single-family homes and larger waterfront properties depending on the pocket, with a lifestyle that leans outdoors and maritime. For many clients, the appeal is simple: water nearby, parks close, and easy access back toward downtown Stamford. It’s a neighborhood that balances Stamford convenience with a coastal edge.
